title: Xtra Dense Korean Cherry Bao
description: A tangy and dense Korean dish featuring cherries and double-rise bread, perfect for curing hangovers, this recipe is inspired by the Chungcheong region and is raw-friendly.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine flour, yeast, salt, and sugar.
Gradually add warm water to the dry ingredients and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it with a damp cloth, and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour.
Punch down the dough and knead it for another 5 minutes.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces and shape each piece into a ball.
Flatten each ball into a disk and place a few chopped cherries in the center of each disk.
Fold the dough over the cherries and seal the edges by pressing them together with a fork.
Brush the tops of the buns with sesame oil and sprinkle with chopped green onions.
Let the buns rise for another 30 minutes before baking them in a preheated oven at 375 degrees for 18 minutes.
